name: lemma_write_seq_first_external_body
reason: `lemma_write_seq_first` is `external_body` with `unimplemented!()` — the inductive decomposition of `write_seq` (popping the first element) is trusted without proof

name: writeback_preserves_writer_mem_via_external
reason: Writer memory preservation across writeback depends on the unverified `lemma_write_seq_first` — if the fold decomposition is wrong, writeback could silently change the writer's view

name: writeback_only_changes_pt_mem_at_addr
code:
proof fn phi_3_writeback_only_changes_pt_mem_at_addr(pre: State, post: State, c: Constants, core: Core, lbl: Lbl, other_addr: usize)
    requires
        step_Writeback(pre, post, c, core, lbl),
        other_addr != pre.sbuf[core][0].0,
        pre.pt_mem.mem.contains_key(other_addr),
    ensures
        post.pt_mem.mem[other_addr] == pre.pt_mem.mem[other_addr],
{
}
reason: Writeback should only modify the drained address — tests that `Map::insert` doesn't affect other keys in the global page table memory

name: writeback_non_writer_core_empty_sbuf
code:
proof fn phi_4_writeback_non_writer_core_empty_sbuf(pre: State, post: State, c: Constants, core: Core, lbl: Lbl, other_core: Core)
    requires
        step_Writeback(pre, post, c, core, lbl),
        pre.inv_sbuf_facts(c),
        c.valid_core(other_core),
        other_core != pre.writes.core,
    ensures
        post.core_mem(other_core).mem[pre.sbuf[core][0].0] == pre.sbuf[core][0].1,
{
}
reason: After writeback, non-writer cores (with empty sbufs) see the newly committed value — tests that writeback makes the write globally visible to other cores

name: writer_sbuf_unique_implies_no_duplicate_addrs
code:
proof fn phi_5_writer_sbuf_unique_implies_no_duplicate_addrs(pre: State, i: int, j: int)
    requires
        pre.writer_sbuf_entries_are_unique(),
        0 <= i < pre.writer_sbuf().len(),
        0 <= j < pre.writer_sbuf().len(),
        pre.writer_sbuf()[i].0 == pre.writer_sbuf()[j].0,
    ensures
        i == j,
{
}
reason: `writer_sbuf_entries_are_unique` should enforce injectivity of addresses — tests that duplicate addresses in the store buffer are impossible
